LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD KANNA MOTION TO VACATE RENEWAL OF JUDGMENT AND TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT FOR LACK OF ACTUAL NOTICE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED. MOTION NOT MADE WITHIN A "REASONABLE TIME." THE JUDGMENT IN THIS CASE HAS BEEN ON FILE FOR OVER TEN YEARS. INSUFFICIENT DEMONSTRATION OF LACK OF PROPER SERVICE. THE EXPIRATION OF THE BUSINESS LICENSE DOES NOT SHOW IMPROPER SERVICE. IT APPEARS THE STORE WAS STILL OPERATING UNDER DEFENDANT'S LIQUOR LICENSE WHEN SERVICE WAS EFFECTUATED, AND ESCROW ON THE SALE OF THE BUSINESS DID NOT CLOSE UNTIL 2000.(PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) calendar.matters:
Renotice Of Date For Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order Granting The Deft'S Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Mar-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF MOLALLA HOLDINGS, INC.'s Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order Granting The Deft'S Motion To Tax Costs is Denied. Commissioner: Paul Slavit, not reported.

Notice And Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order Granting The Deft'S Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Feb-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MOLALLA HOLDINGS, INC., DBA ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE Motion for Reconsideration of the Court's Order Granting the Defendant's Motion to Tax Costs. (Due to the unavailability of Judge Woolard, all calendar matters this date originating out of Department 302, were called from Department 301, by Judge Busch.) No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar, motion to be directed to the discovery department, which issued the underlying order. (302/CWW) Judge: Peter J. Busch; Court Reporter: Carol Karen CSR # 8189

Notice Of New Hearing On Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Nov-12-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Mohammad M. Kanna's Motion to Tax Costs is GRANTED. Plaintiff Molalla Holdings, Inc. dba Accounts Receivable Management Solutions ("ARMS") request for attorney's fees contained in its memorandum of costs fails to comply with Code of Civil Procedure Sections 685.040 and 685.080(a) and fax and file fees are not recoverable under Code of Civil Procedure Section 685.070. Judge: Andrew Cheng, not reported.

MOTION TO TAX COSTS
Off Calendar Sep-24-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD KANNA MOTION TO TAX COSTS, PURSUANT TO CCP SECTIONS 685.070(C), et al. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar. Motion to be calendared in the Discovery Department per San Francisco Local Rule 10A3. Please call and re-calendar in the Discovery Department. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion To Vacate Renewal Of Judgment And To Vacate Entry Of Default And Default Judgment For Lack Of Actual Notice
Off Calendar Jul-31-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MOHAMMAD KANNA Motion to Vacate Renewal of Judgment and To Vacate Entry of Default and Default Judgment For Lack Of Actual Notice. Appearances: Robert D. Resner, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 482-9500, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Michael L. Mau, Attorney at Law, (415) 495-8082, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. Motion not made within a "reasonable time." The judgment in this case has been on file for over ten years. Insufficient demonstration of lack of proper service. The expiration of the business license does not show improper service. It appears the store was still operating under defendant's liquor license when service was effectuated, and escrow on the sale of the business did not close until 2000.(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
